journal_entry.reason: Capitalize purchase price and costs to bring inventory to present location and condition (inbound freight and insurance) into Inventories — merchandise. Expense post-acquisition storage and outbound delivery costs as period expenses. Record accounts payable for the on-account purchase and reduce cash for all cash payments.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Target purchased merchandise on account for $1,000,000. Under IAS 2.11, the cost of inventories includes all costs of purchase and other costs incurred in bringing the inventories to their present location and condition. Therefore, the inbound freight to warehouse ($120,000) and shipping insurance ($30,000) are capitalized into inventory cost, totaling $1,150,000. The post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) is a period cost per IAS 2.16 (abnormal storage costs are excluded from inventory cost) and is expensed. The outbound delivery freight ($50,000) is a selling/distribution cost and is also expensed as incurred. Cash decreases by the total of all cash payments ($220,000), and accounts payable increases by the merchandise purchase amount ($1,000,000).
